channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: All along Kenilworth Road. Both sides, 14th June
item.description:
There is lots of litter all along Kenilworth Road. Pavement and road need sweeping plus flower beds. Been like this for some time

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kenilworth Road, London

item.title: 60 Beondesbury Road, 14th June
item.description:
I would like to report a concern regarding the trees located on the public highway outside and adjacent to my property. Over time, these trees have become significantly overgrown, and I am concerned that their root systems may be affecting the foundations of my property. In recent years, I have noticed the appearance and progression of cracks on the internal walls of the house, which I believe may be linked to ground movement associated with the nearby trees. The trees are now very large and in close proximity to the property. Given the increasing size of the cracks and the potential risk of further structural damage, I would be grateful if the Council could arrange for an inspection of the trees and assess whether maintenance, pruning, root management, or any other remedial action may be necessary. I would appreciate guidance on the appropriate process for investigating this matter and any information the Council may require from me to support the assessment. Thank you for your attention to this issue. I look forward to your response.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unknown substance spotted on ground, 14th June
item.description:
Spotted the photographed substance (grey pellets) near willow tree in Whitehall Recreation Ground (Union Road entrance). We couldn't identify it so are reporting because of known incidents in Beckenham area recently.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Walpole Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Peabody continue to mismanage their bins, 14th June
item.description:
You report that this problem is over but it is not. This was taken two days after you emailed me to say the case was closed. Rats and foxes are often seen at this place.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lanes, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
